page: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-24-lssd is a memory-optimized server instance from the c4 family. It features 24 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 12 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor running at 2.3 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The server is equipped with 186.0 GB of RAM, providing 7.75 GB of memory per core, and a large 504 MB L3 cache. Local storage is bundled, consisting of 1343 GB of NVMe SSD capacity across four local drives. No GPU accelerators are included.

Performance benchmarks indicate exceptional single-core capabilities, with Geekbench single-core and text processing scores reaching the top 10% tier. Single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression also rank in the top 10%. Multi-core performance, including Geekbench multi-core and bzip3 multi-threaded decompression, falls into the top 25% tier. However, memory bandwidth and database operations perform in the middle 50% average tier. For machine learning, the CPU's Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) support strong text generation speeds for small 135M models, though performance drops significantly to the bottom 10% when running text generation on large 70B models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, this instance offers a high density of memory and fast local NVMe storage relative to its compute resources, making it a viable alternative to GPU-accelerated instances for workloads that do not require hardware acceleration. The combination of high single-core performance, substantial memory, and fast local storage makes this server well-suited for memory-intensive databases, text processing pipelines, web serving, and small-scale language model inference.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-24-lssd is a memory-optimized server powered by an Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor with 24 dedicated vCPUs and 12 physical cores operating at 2.3 GHz. It features 186.0 GB of RAM alongside 1343 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, with no GPU acceleration. Benchmarks show top-tier single-core performance, particularly in text processing and single-threaded decompression, alongside strong multi-core execution. While memory bandwidth and database operations sit in the average tier, the hardware's Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) enable strong inference speeds for smaller language models. This resource profile offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-heavy and local storage-dependent tasks, making it highly suitable for in-memory databases, text processing, and web serving. The large 504 MB L3 cache further supports data-intensive operations.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-24-lssd is a memory-optimized server with 24 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 186 GB RAM, and 1343 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, optimized for database and text processing workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-24-lssd features 24 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 186 GB RAM, and 1343 GB local NVMe SSD storage for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized instance combining dedicated Intel Xeon processing, high-speed local NVMe storage, and exceptional single-core performance for data-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 24 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs with 186 GB RAM (7.75 GB per core)
1343 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier single-core performance in text processing and decompression
Intel AMX support for accelerated small-model LLM inference
Best suited for in-memory databases, web serving, and ETL pipelines
